title: Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister, puts social justice at top of agenda
subhead: The Makerfield MP has been declared Labour leader and is set to succeed Keir Starmer on Monday. His platform remains vague and he will have to contend with tight public finances.

title: Germany to join French military nuclear exercise, Merz says
Why Russia's hybrid warfare is forcing France to raise its voice
Elie Barnavi: 'Marwan Barghouti is the only one capable of uniting the Palestinian people around a national project and the two-state solution'
subhead: The former Israeli ambassador to France, in an op-ed for Le Monde, calls for the release of the Palestinian politician, who has been held in Israel since 2002. He argues that Barghouti has consistently championed the project of a sovereign Palestine living in peace alongside Israel.

title: In Argentina, a thriving theater scene
subhead: Buenos Aires has emerged as the epicenter of Spanish-language theater, where a vibrant creative scene – both precarious and remarkably prolific – is defined by the generosity of its performances.

title: BTS, the South Korean group that propelled K-pop to the top of the global music industry
subhead: As the seven artists perform on July 17 and 18 at the Stade de France, we look back at their beginnings in 2013 and their unprecedented rise.

title: 19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhead: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

title: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
subhead: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'
